enrichment.short_description: Open-source intranet and community platform with a free self-hosted edition plus paid Professional and Enterprise editions.
enrichment.starting_price: From €36/month (Professional Edition, hosted SaaS base tier)
enrichment.seo_meta_description: HumHub is an open-source intranet and community platform with a free self-hosted edition plus paid Professional and Enterprise plans.

features.title: People profiles
features.description: User profiles and a searchable member directory.

features.title: Spaces
features.description: Dedicated areas for teams, projects, or interest groups.

features.title: Content tools
features.description: Posts, file sharing, and wiki pages inside each space.

features.title: 80+ modules
features.description: Marketplace extensions such as Wiki, Calendar, Messenger, and SAML-SSO.

features.title: Granular permissions
features.description: Configurable access control across spaces and content.

features.title: Full-text search
features.description: Search across posts, files, and spaces.

features.title: Dashboard and notifications
features.description: Central activity feed and notification system.

pros: Free, self-hostable Community Edition with full source access
80+ optional modules for extending functionality
Choice of on-premise or GDPR-compliant EU SaaS hosting
Transparent, monthly-cancellable SaaS billing with no lock-in between hosting types
cons: Premium support, commercial license, and exclusive modules require a paid Professional or Enterprise edition
SaaS pricing scales with user count and requires configuration on the pricing page
Enterprise-tier pricing is custom/quote-based

faqs.question: Is HumHub free to use?
faqs.answer: The Community Edition is free and open source for self-hosting. Professional and Enterprise editions add paid support, hosting, and extra modules.

faqs.question: Can HumHub be self-hosted?
faqs.answer: Yes, both the free Community Edition and the paid Professional Edition support on-premise deployment, alongside an optional SaaS hosting option.

faqs.question: What does the Professional SaaS plan cost?
faqs.answer: Pricing starts around €36/month and scales with the number of user accounts and the chosen onboarding package, billed monthly or annually.

faqs.question: Where is HumHub based?
faqs.answer: HumHub GmbH & Co. KG is headquartered in Munich, Germany.

use_cases.title: Company intranet
use_cases.description: Centralize internal communication, files, and team spaces for an organization.

use_cases.title: Nonprofit and public sector collaboration
use_cases.description: Provide a GDPR-compliant collaboration hub for nonprofits and public institutions.

use_cases.title: University community platform
use_cases.description: Support student and staff collaboration in an academic setting.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is HumHub?
paragraphs: HumHub is an open-source intranet and community collaboration platform built around four pillars: People, Spaces, Content, and Modules. Organizations use it to run internal social intranets, project spaces, and knowledge hubs.

It ships as a free, self-hostable Community Edition, with paid Professional and Enterprise editions adding hosted SaaS options, premium support, a commercial license, and access to additional modules.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who HumHub is for
paragraphs: HumHub is aimed at companies, nonprofits, public institutions, and universities that want an intranet or community platform they can either self-host for free or run as a supported, hosted service.

enrichment.short_description: Mastodon is free, open-source, decentralized microblogging software built on the ActivityPub federation protocol.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Mastodon is free, open-source, decentralized microblogging software that lets anyone join or self-host a federated social network.

features.title: Federated network
features.description: Connects independently operated servers into the ActivityPub-based fediverse.

features.title: Chronological timeline
features.description: No algorithmic ranking or ad-driven feed manipulation.

features.title: Rich media posts
features.description: Support for images, video, audio attachments, and polls.

features.title: Accessibility tools
features.description: Content warnings and image alt-text descriptions.

features.title: Custom emoji
features.description: Server-specific custom emoji support.

features.title: Self-hosting
features.description: Run an independent instance with full server-level moderation control.

features.title: Official mobile apps
features.description: Official iOS and Android apps for accessing Mastodon.

pros: Free and open-source software (AGPL-3.0)
No advertising or algorithmic feed manipulation
Fully self-hostable for complete control over data and moderation
Official iOS and Android apps in addition to web access
Interoperates with other ActivityPub fediverse platforms
cons: No official managed hosting sold directly by the Mastodon project
Self-hosting an instance requires server administration skills
Sustained by donations and sponsors rather than a dedicated commercial support tier

faqs.question: Is Mastodon free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Mastodon is entirely free and open-source software, sustained by donations rather than paid tiers.

faqs.question: Can I self-host Mastodon?
faqs.answer: Yes, anyone can self-host their own Mastodon instance, or join an existing public server such as mastodon.social.

faqs.question: What license is Mastodon released under?
faqs.answer: Mastodon's source code is released under the AGPL-3.0 license.

faqs.question: Does Mastodon have mobile apps?
faqs.answer: Yes, official iOS and Android apps are available in addition to the web interface.

use_cases.title: Personal microblogging
use_cases.description: Post and follow others on the open, ad-free social web.
use_cases.audience: Individuals seeking a decentralized microblogging platform

use_cases.title: Community-run instances
use_cases.description: Organizations or communities can self-host their own moderated Mastodon server.
use_cases.audience: Communities and organizations wanting to self-host social media

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Mastodon?
paragraphs: Mastodon is free, open-source microblogging software launched in 2016 by Eugen Rochko. Instead of one central company, Mastodon runs as thousands of independently operated servers that interconnect via the ActivityPub protocol, collectively known as the fediverse.
Users can join an existing public server or self-host their own, with each server setting its own moderation rules while still being able to follow and interact with accounts on other servers.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Mastodon is for
paragraphs: Mastodon suits individuals who want an ad-free, non-algorithmic social feed, as well as communities and organizations that want to self-host and moderate their own corner of the social web.

enrichment.short_description: Misskey is free, open-source software for running a decentralized, ActivityPub-based social network.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Misskey is free, open-source, self-hosted social networking software with ActivityPub federation and a highly customizable UI.

features.title: ActivityPub federation
features.description: Connects self-hosted instances to the wider fediverse.

features.title: Deck UI
features.description: Customizable, column-based layout for organizing content.

features.title: MFM rich text
features.description: Misskey Flavored Markdown for expressive note formatting.

features.title: Custom emoji and themes
features.description: Server-level custom emoji, reactions, and visual themes.

features.title: Channels and antennas
features.description: Tools for organizing and filtering content by topic.

features.title: Two-factor authentication
features.description: Built-in account security and privacy controls.

features.title: REST API
features.description: Full API access on a Node.js backend with PostgreSQL.

pros: Free and open-source software (AGPL-3.0)
Highly customizable interface (Deck UI, themes, custom emoji)
Actively developed, with over 1,300 releases on GitHub
Full API access for building integrations and clients
cons: Maintained by a small volunteer team relying on donations
No official managed hosting; requires self-hosting or joining a third-party instance
Self-hosting requires Node.js/PostgreSQL server administration

faqs.question: Is Misskey free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Misskey is fully free and open-source software.

faqs.question: What license does Misskey use?
faqs.answer: Misskey is released under the AGPL-3.0 license.

faqs.question: Who created Misskey?
faqs.answer: Misskey was created by developer syuilo, with the project dating back to 2014.

faqs.question: Can I run my own Misskey server?
faqs.answer: Yes, Misskey supports self-hosting, including Docker-based deployment.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted fediverse server
use_cases.description: Run an independent, customizable social networking server.

use_cases.title: Personal decentralized microblogging
use_cases.description: Post and interact across the fediverse with a highly customizable client.
use_cases.audience: Individuals wanting a customizable fediverse experience

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Misskey?
paragraphs: Misskey is free, open-source software for building decentralized, ActivityPub-based social networking services, in development since 2014 and maintained by the Misskey Project.
It is known for a highly customizable Deck UI, custom emoji and themes, and MFM (Misskey Flavored Markdown) for rich-text notes, alongside standard fediverse federation with platforms like Mastodon.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Misskey is for
paragraphs: Misskey is suited to server administrators and communities who want to self-host a customizable fediverse instance, and to developers building on its API.

enrichment.short_description: Pleroma is a free, open-source, lightweight fediverse server that federates with Mastodon, Pixelfed, and Misskey.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Pleroma is a free, open-source, lightweight fediverse server with multiple frontend options and ActivityPub federation.

features.title: Lightweight architecture
features.description: Designed to run efficiently, suited to smaller servers.

features.title: ActivityPub federation
features.description: Interoperates with Mastodon, Pixelfed, Misskey, and other fediverse software.

features.title: Multiple frontends
features.description: Choice of Pleroma FE, Soapbox, or Glitch-lily interfaces.

features.title: Customizable UX
features.description: Configurable frontend behavior and appearance.

features.title: Self-hosting
features.description: Install and run your own instance on your own infrastructure.

pros: Free and open-source software (AGPL-3.0)
Lightweight compared to some other fediverse server software
Multiple frontend options to suit different user preferences
Interoperable with Mastodon, Pixelfed, and Misskey via ActivityPub
cons: Some public instances have closed new registrations due to low staffing
Development is community/volunteer-driven and sustained by donations
No official managed hosting sold by the project

faqs.question: Is Pleroma free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Pleroma is free, open-source software.

faqs.question: What license does Pleroma use?
faqs.answer: Pleroma is released under the AGPL-3.0 license.

faqs.question: Can I self-host Pleroma?
faqs.answer: Yes, Pleroma is designed to be self-hosted; you can also join an existing public instance.

faqs.question: Does Pleroma federate with Mastodon?
faqs.answer: Yes, Pleroma is compatible with other fediverse software including Mastodon, Pixelfed, and Misskey via ActivityPub.

use_cases.title: Lightweight self-hosted instance
use_cases.description: Run a resource-efficient fediverse server for a small community.
use_cases.audience: Server admins with limited hosting resources

use_cases.title: Custom frontend deployment
use_cases.description: Choose or build a frontend tailored to a community's needs.
use_cases.audience: Communities wanting a customized fediverse experience

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Pleroma?
paragraphs: Pleroma is a free, open-source fediverse server described by its maintainers as lightweight, built to enable free and open communication through the ActivityPub protocol.
It supports multiple frontends, including the customizable Pleroma FE, Soapbox, and Glitch-lily, and interoperates with other fediverse platforms such as Mastodon, Pixelfed, and Misskey.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Pleroma is for
paragraphs: Pleroma suits server administrators who want a resource-efficient fediverse server and communities that value frontend flexibility.

enrichment.short_description: Friendica is a free, open-source decentralized social network federating via ActivityPub, OStatus, and diaspora*.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Friendica is free, open-source decentralized social networking software supporting ActivityPub, OStatus, and diaspora* federation.

features.title: Multi-protocol federation
features.description: Interoperates via ActivityPub, OStatus, and diaspora* protocols.

features.title: Access controls
features.description: Fine-grained privacy settings on posts and private group conversations.

features.title: Photo sharing and events
features.description: Core posting, photo sharing, and event organization tools.

features.title: Email integration
features.description: Connects via IMAP4rev1/ESMTP for email-based communication.

features.title: RSS/Atom feeds
features.description: Feed integration for following external content.

features.title: Plugins and themes
features.description: Extensible via third-party plugins and custom themes.

pros: Free and open-source software (AGPLv3)
Runs on standard PHP/MySQL hosting stacks
Interoperates across multiple federation protocols, not just ActivityPub
Long-running project active since 2010 with regular releases
cons: Self-hosting requires PHP/MySQL server administration
Relies on volunteer development rather than a commercial vendor
Feature set spans several legacy protocols, which can add configuration complexity

faqs.question: Is Friendica free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Friendica is free, open-source software.

faqs.question: What license does Friendica use?
faqs.answer: Friendica is released under the AGPLv3 (or later) license.

faqs.question: Can Friendica connect with Mastodon?
faqs.answer: Yes, Friendica federates with Mastodon, Pixelfed, and other ActivityPub-based platforms.

faqs.question: What server software does Friendica need?
faqs.answer: Friendica runs on a standard PHP and MySQL hosting stack.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted social network
use_cases.description: Run a personal or community server that connects to multiple federation protocols.
use_cases.audience: Individuals and communities familiar with self-hosting

use_cases.title: Cross-protocol communication hub
use_cases.description: Bridge ActivityPub, OStatus, diaspora*, email, and RSS content in one place.
use_cases.audience: Users wanting a single hub for varied feeds and networks

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Friendica?
paragraphs: Friendica is a free, open-source decentralized social network that has been in development since 2010. It federates across multiple protocols, including ActivityPub, OStatus, and diaspora*, and also integrates with email and RSS/Atom feeds.
It runs on standard PHP and MySQL infrastructure, and its official description notes that if you can run WordPress, you can run Friendica.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Friendica is for
paragraphs: Friendica is aimed at individuals and communities comfortable with self-hosting who want a social network that bridges several federation protocols rather than just ActivityPub.

enrichment.short_description: Pixelfed is a free, open-source, decentralized photo-sharing platform built on ActivityPub, started in 2018.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Pixelfed is a free, open-source, federated photo-sharing network with official iOS and Android apps and no algorithmic feed.

features.title: ActivityPub federation
features.description: Connects independent Pixelfed and fediverse instances.

features.title: Chronological feed
features.description: Photo timeline without algorithmic ranking.

features.title: Self-hosting
features.description: Run your own independent instance with full control.

features.title: Official mobile apps
features.description: Official iOS and Android apps, launched in January 2025.

features.title: Hosted flagship instance
features.description: pixelfed.social is available as a ready-to-use public instance.

features.title: Multi-language support
features.description: Translated via the Crowdin localization platform.

pros: Free and open-source software (AGPL-3.0)
Official iOS and Android apps in addition to the web app
No algorithmic feed manipulation or advertising
Backed by sponsors including the NLnet Foundation, Fastly, and DigitalOcean
cons: Self-hosting requires Laravel/PHP server administration
No official managed paid hosting sold directly by the Pixelfed project
Development is driven largely by a small core team

faqs.question: Is Pixelfed free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Pixelfed is free, open-source software.

faqs.question: What license does Pixelfed use?
faqs.answer: Pixelfed is released under the AGPL-3.0 license.

faqs.question: Who created Pixelfed?
faqs.answer: Pixelfed was started by developer Daniel Supernault (dansup) in 2018.

faqs.question: Does Pixelfed have a mobile app?
faqs.answer: Yes, official iOS and Android apps launched in January 2025.

use_cases.title: Decentralized photo sharing
use_cases.description: Share photos on a federated, ad-free, chronological-feed platform.
use_cases.audience: Photographers and individuals seeking an Instagram alternative

use_cases.title: Self-hosted community instance
use_cases.description: Run an independent, moderated photo-sharing server for a community.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Pixelfed?
paragraphs: Pixelfed is a free, open-source, decentralized photo-sharing platform built on the ActivityPub protocol, started by Daniel Supernault in 2018.

It offers a chronological, non-algorithmic feed, official iOS and Android apps launched in January 2025, and a hosted flagship instance at pixelfed.social, alongside the option to self-host an independent instance.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Pixelfed is for
paragraphs: Pixelfed suits individuals looking for an ad-free, algorithm-free photo-sharing alternative, as well as communities and admins who want to self-host their own federated photo server.

enrichment.short_description: Lemmy is a free, open-source, federated link-aggregation and discussion platform built in Rust, a Reddit alternative.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Lemmy is a free, open-source, federated Reddit alternative built with Rust and self-hostable via Docker or Ansible.

features.title: Voting and threaded comments
features.description: Upvote/downvote system with nested comment discussions.

features.title: Federated communities
features.description: Interconnected instances forming the 'Lemmyverse' via ActivityPub.

features.title: Multi-platform apps
features.description: Apps available across iOS, Android, desktop, and web.

features.title: Low resource usage
features.description: Rust backend keeps hosting requirements low enough to run on a Raspberry Pi.

features.title: Customizable themes
features.description: User and community tagging with emoji support, plus theme customization.

features.title: 30+ interface languages
features.description: Supports more than 30 languages in its user interface.

features.title: RSS/Atom and email notifications
features.description: Feed export and email notification support.

pros: Free and open-source software (AGPL-3.0)
No advertising or user tracking
Very low hosting resource requirements thanks to Rust
Funded solely by donations, independent of venture or corporate backing
cons: Self-hosting requires Docker/Ansible setup and server administration
No official managed paid hosting sold by the project
Relies on volunteer development and community donations for sustainability

faqs.question: Is Lemmy free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Lemmy is free and non-commercial, with no ads or tracking, funded solely by donations.

faqs.question: What license does Lemmy use?
faqs.answer: Lemmy is released under the AGPL-3.0 license.

faqs.question: Can Lemmy run on low-power hardware?
faqs.answer: Yes, its Rust backend keeps resource usage low enough to run on a small Raspberry Pi.

faqs.question: Are there mobile apps for Lemmy?
faqs.answer: Yes, apps are available across iOS, Android, desktop, and web.

use_cases.title: Community discussion forums
use_cases.description: Host link-sharing and discussion communities as a Reddit alternative.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted federated instance
use_cases.description: Run a low-resource, independent instance connected to the wider Lemmyverse.
use_cases.audience: Server admins with limited hosting budgets

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Lemmy?
paragraphs: Lemmy is a free, open-source, federated link-aggregation and discussion platform, functioning as a decentralized alternative to Reddit. Development began in 2019, and the software is built in Rust for low resource usage.
Lemmy instances federate with each other via ActivityPub, forming the 'Lemmyverse,' and the project is funded solely by donations rather than advertising or venture capital.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Lemmy is for
paragraphs: Lemmy is suited to communities and admins who want to run a link-aggregation/discussion forum without ads or tracking, including on low-cost or low-power hosting.

name: Kbin
skip_reason: Kbin's lead developer stopped active development, the flagship kbin.social instance has shut down, and the project's work has been superseded by the community fork Mbin. The project is effectively discontinued, so content cannot be enriched accurately as a currently-maintained tool.

enrichment.short_description: Mobilizon is free, open-source, federated software for creating, sharing, and finding events, originally built by Framasoft.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Mobilizon is free, open-source, federated event-organizing software originally built by Framasoft and now maintained by Kaihuri.

features.title: Event creation and sharing
features.description: Create, edit, and share events with a public or private audience.

features.title: Group organization
features.description: Manage groups and coordinate members around shared events.

features.title: Search and discovery
features.description: Find events by keyword, location, or date.

features.title: Moderation and roles
features.description: User roles distinguishing registered users from visitors, plus moderation tools.

features.title: API and feeds
features.description: API access with ICS and RSS feed export.

features.title: ActivityPub federation
features.description: Federates with other Mobilizon and fediverse instances.

features.title: Multilingual support
features.description: Available in 20+ languages.

pros: Free and open-source software (AGPL-3.0), with publicly auditable source code on Framagit
Federated via ActivityPub rather than tied to one company's servers
Originally built by Framasoft and now maintained by the non-profit Kaihuri, with NLnet funding support
Purpose-built for event organizing rather than a general social feed
cons: No public, self-serve pricing for managed hosting; professional hosting is arranged by contacting the team directly
Self-hosting requires server administration
Maintenance responsibility transitioned between organizations (Framasoft to Kaihuri in 2024), which can affect long-term roadmap continuity

faqs.question: Is Mobilizon free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Mobilizon is free, open-source software.

faqs.question: What license does Mobilizon use?
faqs.answer: Mobilizon is released under the AGPL-3.0 license.

faqs.question: Who built Mobilizon?
faqs.answer: Mobilizon was originally created by the French non-profit Framasoft, with maintenance handed over to the non-profit Kaihuri in 2024.

faqs.question: Can I get hosted Mobilizon without self-hosting?
faqs.answer: The project points users to contact the team for professional hosting services; no public self-serve pricing is listed.

use_cases.title: Community event organizing
use_cases.description: Create and share events for local clubs, activist groups, or associations without relying on a corporate social network.

use_cases.title: Federated event discovery
use_cases.description: Find events across federated Mobilizon instances by keyword, location, or date.
use_cases.audience: Individuals looking for local or community events

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Mobilizon?
paragraphs: Mobilizon is free, open-source software for creating, sharing, and finding events, publicly launched in October 2020 by the French non-profit Framasoft. Maintenance was handed over to the non-profit Kaihuri in 2024.
It federates via ActivityPub, so events and groups on one Mobilizon instance can be discovered and interacted with from other instances, and its source code is publicly auditable on Framagit.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Mobilizon is for
paragraphs: Mobilizon is aimed at communities, activist groups, and associations that want to organize and promote events without depending on a corporate social media platform.

enrichment.short_description: Pretix is open-source event ticketing software with a free self-hosted edition and a hosted SaaS priced per ticket.
enrichment.starting_price: 2.5% per paid ticket (max €15/ticket) on the hosted SaaS; self-hosted software is free and open source
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Pretix is open-source event ticketing software with a free self-hosted AGPLv3 edition and a hosted SaaS priced per ticket sold.

features.title: Customizable ticket shop
features.description: Branded online ticket shop with a built-in marketing toolkit.

features.title: Payment provider choice
features.description: Direct integration with providers including PayPal, Stripe, and Mollie.

features.title: pretixPOS
features.description: Point-of-sale/cash register module for on-site ticket sales.

features.title: pretixSCAN
features.description: Access control and badge printing for check-in.

features.title: pretixKIOSK
features.description: Self-service ticket vending module.

features.title: Venueless integration
features.description: Add-on for running digital or hybrid event components.

features.title: Reporting and statistics
features.description: Built-in sales reports and event statistics.

pros: Self-hosted core is free and open source (AGPLv3)
Hosted SaaS has no monthly subscription fee, only pay-per-ticket pricing
First 500 free tickets per year at no charge on the hosted plan
Modular add-ons (POS, scanning, kiosk) for on-site event operations
No long-term contract required
cons: Hosted pricing (2.5% of ticket price, capped at €15, plus payment fees) can add up for high-value tickets
The free-ticket quota (500/year) only grows further with paid ticket sales
Add-on services such as lead scanning and e-invoicing carry extra per-unit fees

faqs.question: Is pretix free?
faqs.answer: The self-hosted core software is free and open source under AGPLv3. The hosted SaaS charges 2.5% per paid ticket (capped at €15) plus payment provider fees, with a limited free tier for free tickets.

faqs.question: Where is pretix based?
faqs.answer: pretix GmbH is headquartered in Heidelberg, Germany.

faqs.question: Can pretix be self-hosted?
faqs.answer: Yes, the open-source core (AGPLv3) can be self-hosted with the same feature set as the hosted service.

faqs.question: How does pretix charge for free tickets?
faqs.answer: The first 500 free tickets issued per year on the hosted SaaS are free; additional free tickets cost €0.50 each.

use_cases.title: Conference and festival ticketing
use_cases.description: Sell and manage tickets for conferences, festivals, and concerts.

use_cases.title: On-site check-in and access control
use_cases.description: Use pretixSCAN and pretixPOS for badge printing, entry control, and on-site sales.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Pretix?
paragraphs: Pretix is open-source ticketing software for conferences, festivals, exhibitions, museums, and other events, developed since 2013 by pretix GmbH in Heidelberg, Germany.

The core software is free and self-hostable under the AGPLv3 license. Organizers who prefer not to self-host can use pretix's hosted SaaS, which charges no monthly fee but takes a percentage of paid ticket sales plus payment provider fees.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Pretix is for
paragraphs: Pretix suits event organizers who need online ticket sales alongside on-site tools like point-of-sale, access control, and badge printing, whether they prefer to self-host or use the hosted service.
